refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: to support achieving a defined cycle time target for a specific operational cycle. It is already focused on what the system must do (support achieving the cycle time target) without specifying how, thus it is solution-neutral and atomic.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Optimize dump mechanism speed and efficiency
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time
Implement cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to meet target
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time

potential_sources: Interdependence of load, travel, and dump timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination as the DP focuses on managing interactions to minimize cycle time without tightly coupling individual mechanisms, supporting independence.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time can be improved primarily through operational coordination rather than hardware redesign
Site conditions and safety policies allow for operational adjustments
Measurement of cycle time is accurate and timely
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for real-time cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to ensure meeting the p95 ≤ CT_target acceptance criteria under varying conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for coordinating load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time across vehicles and teleoperation services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges operational data and status updates with vehicles and teleoperation systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends coordinated task commands to vehicles and teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety alerts from Safety Services to adjust operation coordination.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication between distributed modules and external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as it directly manages coordination logic; minimal diffusion since coordination is centralized here.
Partial containment as vehicles execute operations but do not coordinate; high diffusion as coordination logic would spread across vehicles.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not overall coordination; high diffusion if coordination logic placed here.
Very low containment; safety services monitor safety but do not coordinate operations; high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP because it centralizes the coordination of load, travel, and dump operations, minimizing idle time effectively with minimal diffusion of coordination logic outside this module.
containment_risks: Potential complexity growth in NGHE Solution as it handles multiple coordination tasks.
Dependency on timely and reliable interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert) to external modules may affect coordination effectiveness.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying coordination of load, travel, and dump operations to minimize idle time, which directly supports achieving the customer-defined cycle time target. The DP is well-aligned with the FR's performance goal and operational context.

diffusion_risks: Coordination logic diffusion risk is low as the NGHE Solution module centralizes the DP implementation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating effective packaging of the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership boundary. However, complexity growth and interface dependencies are noted risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under defined duty cycles and site conditions, as well as ensuring compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and unambiguous, with well-defined external interfaces supporting coordination functions.

recommended_revisions: Monitor complexity growth within the NGHE Solution module to maintain maintainability and performance.
Establish robust interface contracts and reliability measures for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert to mitigate dependency risks.
Consider modularizing coordination sub-functions within NGHE Solution if complexity increases to preserve cohesion and manageability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong alignment and clear ownership boundaries. The DP logically addresses the FR, the NGHE Solution module effectively contains the DP with low diffusion risk, and verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although complexity growth and interface dependency risks exist, they are manageable through monitoring and interface contracts without requiring further decomposition at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and well-contained module is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Monitor complexity growth and interface dependencies within the NGHE Solution module to maintain maintainability and safety compliance, but no immediate revision to FR or DP is needed.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will continue to effectively contain the DP.
Interface contracts and reliability measures can mitigate dependency risks.
Verification methods for cycle time and safety compliance are sufficient.
open_risks: Complexity growth within NGHE Solution module may impact maintainability and performance.
Interface dependencies on Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert modules may introduce reliability risks.
evidence_needed: Ongoing monitoring data on module complexity and performance.
Verification results of cycle time p95 against CT_target under operational conditions.
Interface reliability and failure rate statistics.
